questions.question: What is the groundspeed in the climb?
questions.context: Use the following scenario for the next five questions: You're flying from Denver (5434') to Chicago (680') which is 915 SM on a TC of 093°. Variation is 5° east. The performance of the jet aircraft and winds aloft are: [[TABLE]]

rows: 315 KTAS at 1,800 FPM up to FL350
Mach .78 (450 KTAS), burning 5,000 LBS per hour

questions.question: How many miles did the aircraft fly in cruise?

questions.question: The miles of flight during cruise will be
questions.context: Use the following to answer questions 27 - 31. [[NEXT_LINE]] You're departing Hometown airport (elevation 2280') with a 400 fpm rate of climb to a cruise altitude of 6500 feet. Your groundspeed in the climb is 85 kts and in cruise it is 120 kts. You descend to 1000 feet above destination airport (elevation 1700) with a groundspeed of 140 kts and a descent rate of 650 fpm. Your fuel consumption is 11 gph in climb, 9.5 gph in cruise and 7 gph in descent. You are carrying 27 gallons of usable fuel. The total distance of the flight is 295 nm.

questions.question: How many nautical miles will you spend in cruise? (Write your answer on the answer sheet to the nearest mile.)
questions.context: Use the following information to answer the next 4 questions. You are embarking on a cross country flight from airport A (elev. 260') to airport B (elev. 850'). The true course is 61° and you will cover 206 statute miles. The wind is from a steady 310° at 26 knots for all phases of flight and you will cruise at 5,500' MSL. [[TABLE]]

questions.question: You takeoff using the calculations from question 27. Just prior to the halfway point the CEO comes up to the flight deck and asks, "What if we changed altitudes at the halfway point to 30,000 feet from 36,000 feet? Can we get there faster?" If the change is instantaneous, you say:

choices.text: Yes, we would get there 3 minutes faster.
Yes, we would get there 8 minutes faster.
Yes, we would get there 13 minutes faster.
No, we would be there 3 minutes later

questions.correctChoice: Yes, we would get there 13 minutes faster.
questions.context: NIKE is interviewing you for a position flying their Gulfstream V. They are asking you some hypothetical questions to gauge your response. Some background information is that you have 13,000 lbs of fuel onboard. The interviewer says for this flight the GV burns fuel at 2000 lbs/hr (above 32,000 feet) and 2200 lbs/hr (below 32,000 feet). Flying at 0.78M with the following winds aloft: [[TABLE]] You are traveling 2120 miles from Hillsboro, OR (HIO) to Honolulu, HI (HNL). The True Course from HIO to HNL is 232°. [[DIVIDER]] Referenced question 27: Using the above information, your next selection is to look at 36,000 feet. What is the time en route and fuel burn, for the entire flight?

questions.question: You plan the flight for the King Air on Route 2 as follows: The King Air climbs at 900 feet per minute to FL240 with a true airspeed of 180 knots and descends at 1500 feet per minute from FL240 with a true airspeed of 260 knots. The winds aloft are calm. What is the distance in cruise for the entirety of Route 2?
questions.context: You are flying for a corporation that has many corporate aircraft. On two separate days in two separate aircraft (a G IV and King Air), you are asked to fly from Madison, WI (800 feet MSL) to Rapid City, SD (2200 feet MSL). You fly in a standard atmosphere unless otherwise stated. Route 1 is with the G IV and Route 2 is with the King Air: [[TABLE]]

rows: MSN to RAP direct (602 nautical miles)
G IV burns 2000 pounds of Jet A per hour and travels at Mach .77 (-45° C)
Leg 1 (MSN to DSM), Leg 2 (DSM to RAP): 294 nautical miles and 422 nautical miles respectively
King Air burns 750 pounds of Jet A per hour and travels at 230 knots true airspeed
Des Moines, IA (600 feet MSL) is a landing airport

questions.question: You plan the flight for the G IV on Route 1 as follows: The G IV climbs at 2000 feet per minute to FL380 with a true airspeed of 270 knots and descends at 2000 feet per minute from FL380 with an average true airspeed of 420 knots. The winds aloft are calm. What is the distance in cruise for Route 1?
